title: Bind canonical route evidence to reviewed shadow outcomes
goal: Implement the minimal proposal-only shadow capture required by the Vibe-Lab routing-readiness experiment by reusing Grabowski route_evidence and binding it to independently reviewed semantic outcomes with primary evidence references.

acceptance.assertion: Shadow capture consumes existing validated hash-bound Grabowski route_evidence and does not infer canonical routing truth from argv or create a second route identity.
Captured outcome separates process lifecycle from independently reviewed semantic task correctness or decision quality and supports abstention when no semantic outcome is available.
Every non-abstaining semantic outcome is bound to primary evidence references such as CI, diff-bound review, explicit operator decision or bounded Chronik evidence.
Capture has no automatic routing, policy, queue, merge or runtime authority and cannot alter the route of the observed task.
Learning records exclude raw prompts, transcripts, private notes and unrestricted argv payloads; only bounded task-class, risk, context and evidence identities are allowed.
Output can populate Vibe-Lab experiment 2026-07-23_operator-routing-ml-readiness-shadow without granting Vibe-Lab execution authority.
Focused regression tests cover missing route evidence, abstaining outcomes, evidence binding, redaction and non-effect behavior.

metadata.source: User request 2026-07-23 plus live Grabowski/Vibe-Lab readiness audit
metadata.reason: The live task store has substantial execution history but no direct canonical route columns or independently reviewed semantic-outcome columns; process state is not a valid supervised-routing quality label.
